chapters.title: Power Is Durable Advantage, Not Temporary Success

summaryBullets: Treating power as durable advantage rather than short term performance creates clearer judgment.
Strategy improves when people separate a good year from a protected position.
This matters because durability matters because many apparent edges disappear under imitation or price pressure.
A common mistake is calling every successful company powerful before checking the mechanism.
The pattern becomes easier to see when results look strong but rivals can copy the edge too easily.
A practical starting move is to name the source of durability, ask how the edge survives imitation, and separate performance from power.
keyTakeaways: Treating power as durable advantage rather than short term performance creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: name the source of durability
ask how the edge survives imitation
separate performance from power

chapters.title: Scale Economies Can Protect the Leader

summaryBullets: Using scale economies as a power that lowers cost or increases value at larger size creates clearer judgment.
Some advantages become stronger as volume grows.
This matters because durable scale matters because later rivals inherit higher relative cost.
A common mistake is assuming bigger is always better without checking whether scale actually improves economics.
The pattern becomes easier to see when size is rising but cost or customer value is not improving with it.
A practical starting move is to check whether scale changes economics, measure who gains from volume, and look for cost or value improvement at scale.
keyTakeaways: Using scale economies as a power that lowers cost or increases value at larger size creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: check whether scale changes economics
measure who gains from volume
look for cost or value improvement at scale
separate real scale power from simple size
